records.ety: [OE. jogelour, juglur, OF. jogleor, jugleor, jongleor, F. jongleur, fr. L. joculator a jester, joker, fr. joculus a little jest or joke, dim. of jocus jest, joke. See Joke, and cf. Jongleur, Joculator.]

defs: One who juggles; one who practices or exhibits tricks by sleight of hand; one skilled in legerdemain; a conjurer.
quotes: As nimble jugglers that deceive the eye.
Jugglers and impostors do daily delude them.
notes: This sense is now expressed by magician or conjurer.

defs: A person who juggles objects, i. e. who maintains several objects in the air by passing them in turn from one hand to another.
